problem.title: Number of Ways to Assign Edge Weights I

approaches.title: HashMap Depth Tracking with Binary Exponentiation
approaches.summary: Track node depths using a HashMap and compute the result with modular binary exponentiation.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Initial attempt using HashMap to track depths but forgot modulo operation.

revisions.commitComment: binary expo! current approach doesn't work
revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 3558!: binary expo! current approach doesn't work
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Added modular binary exponentiation to handle large numbers.

approaches.title: HashMap Depth Tracking with Sorting and Modulo
approaches.summary: Sort edges by parent node and track depths using HashMap with modulo exponentiation.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Simplified sorting and added min/max to handle edge direction.

approaches.title: DFS Stack-Based Depth Tracking
approaches.summary: Use DFS with a stack to traverse the tree and track maximum depth for result calculation.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Implemented DFS stack to find max depth, replacing HashMap approach.

approaches.title: Array-Based Depth Tracking with Binary Exponentiation
approaches.summary: Track node depths using an array and compute the result with modular binary exponentiation.

revisions.commitComment: wrong, but works and is fast
revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 3558!: wrong, but works and is fast
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Switched to array for depth tracking, removed DFS, but still incorrect for non-sequential graphs.

approaches.title: HashMap with Non-Sequential Graph Handling
approaches.summary: Attempt to fix non-sequential graph handling by adding test cases and adjusting depth tracking.

revisions.commitComment: fails for non sequential graphs (not in test cases)
revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 3558!: fails for non sequential graphs (not in test cases)
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Added test case for non-sequential graphs but approach still fails for such cases.
